title: 8 Herbal Tinctures For Gum Swelling
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great way to relieve gum swelling. You see, when you have gum swelling, it's usually because of inflammation or infection. Herbal teas can help reduce inflammation and fight off the infection, which in turn reduces the swelling. For example, Echinacea purpurea is a herb that's known for its anti-inflammatory properties. It can help calm down the swelling and reduce any pain or discomfort.  Another herb that's great for gum swelling is Eucalyptus globulus. It has natural antibacterial properties that can help fight off any infection in your gums. This can help prevent the swelling from getting worse and promote healing.  Ginger, specifically Zingiber officinale, is also great for gum swelling. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain. It also has antiseptic properties that can help prevent infection.  Using herbal tinctures for gum swelling can have many benefits. For one, they're a natural way to relieve swelling, so you don't have to worry about putting harsh chemicals in your body. They're also a great way to prevent gum swelling from getting worse, which can save you a lot of time and money in the long run. Plus, herbal tinctures can help promote healing, which means your gums will be back to normal in no time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains the bioactive constituents alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the gums. Alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea tinctures have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, substances that promote inflammation. Caffeic acid derivatives and rosmarinic acid have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the gums from damage, Echinacea purpurea tinctures may be a useful treatment for gum swelling.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers. You can buy it online or at a local health store.
Combine the dried Echinacea with 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar daily to help the tincture steep.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Take 20-30 drops of the tincture, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or taken directly under the tongue. Monitor the gum swelling and adjust the dose as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures contains compounds like eucalyptol, camphor, and borneol. These bioactive constituents help reduce inflammation in the gums by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which cause swelling. Eucalyptol also has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections that can cause gum swelling. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of eucalyptus globulus tinctures help soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing swelling and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, eucalyptus globulus tinctures can help alleviate gum swelling and promote healthy gums.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Eucalyptus leaves to the jar and make sure they are completely covered by the vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the leaves.
Store the Eucalyptus globulus tincture in a cool, dark place and use 5-10 drops as needed to relieve gum swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures contains gingerols and shogaols, which are compounds found in the rhizome of the ginger plant. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling. Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol, inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. Shogaols, on the other hand, have antioxidant properties that help combat free radicals, which contribute to inflammation. By reducing inflammation and pain, Zingiber officinale tinctures can be effective in alleviating gum swelling.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Zingiber officinale roots.
Add 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or glycerin to a clean glass jar.
Combine the Zingiber officinale roots with the vodka or glycerin in the jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The triterpenoid saponins help reduce swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The flavonoids in Calendula officinalis have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals. The antiseptic properties of Calendula officinalis tinctures also help prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for gum healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting a clean environment, Calendula officinalis tinctures can help alleviate gum swelling.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers, 2 cups of vodka (at least 80 proof), and a clean glass jar.
Combine the Calendula flowers and vodka in the glass jar. Make sure the flowers are completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-10 drops of the tincture in water to treat gum swelling as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tinctures contains bioactive constituents like rosmarinic acid, bornyl acetate, and carnosic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums. The rosmarinic acid in Salvia officinalis tinctures acts as an antioxidant, which helps to neutralize free radicals that can cause tissue damage and inflammation. The bornyl acetate also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections that can cause gum swelling. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Salvia officinalis tinctures can help to alleviate gum swelling and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, 2 cups of 80% ethanol (vodka or rum work too), and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Place the Salvia officinalis leaves in the glass jar and pour the 80% ethanol over them, making sure the leaves are covered.
Close the jar with the lid and store it in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a smaller glass bottle and store it in the fridge. Use 5-10 drops of the tincture as needed to help with gum swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures contains flavonoids and bilobalide as its primary bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums. Flavonoids also have antioxidant properties that protect against gum damage caused by free radicals. Bilobalide helps to improve blood flow to the gums, reducing swelling and promoting healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Ginkgo biloba tinctures can help to alleviate gum swelling and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can buy this from a health food store or online.
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops of the tincture as needed to treat gum swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains biologically active constituents like aloin and aloe-emodin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the gums. Aloin, in particular, is a powerful anti-inflammatory agent that can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation. Aloe-emodin also has anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce pain and swelling in the gums. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Aloe barbadensis tinctures can help alleviate gum swelling and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis leaves and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Chop the Aloe leaves into small pieces and add them to the jar with vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 5-10 drops as needed to soothe gum swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures contains thymol and carvacrol as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and infection in the gums. Thymol, in particular, has a strong decongestant effect that can help alleviate swelling and promote healing. Carvacrol's antibacterial properties can help fight off the underlying infection causing the swelling. By reducing inflammation and infection, Thymus vulgaris tinctures can help alleviate gum swelling and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal tinctures for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Thymus vulgaris leaves to the jar and pour in the vodka, making sure the leaves are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Thymus vulgaris tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, as needed to treat gum swelling.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gum swelling from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and eucalyptus, have anti-inflammatory properties that could reduce swelling. The active compounds in these teas may also help kill bacteria that cause gum inflammation. Regular consumption might help prevent gum swelling from occurring in the first place.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be helpful for gum swelling, but it's not a good idea to drink them every day. Some herbal teas can be too strong or interact with medications, making gum swelling worse. It's best to drink them in moderation, like a few cups a week, to see if they help.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce gum swelling. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can show results in as little as 24 to 48 hours. However, it may take a few days, up to 3 to 5 days, for the swelling to fully subside. The time it takes depends on the individual and the severity of the swelling.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for gum swelling is best in the morning, after breakfast. The caffeine from the tea can help reduce swelling and ease pain. The warmth from the tea can also help loosen up the gum and make it feel better.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for gum swelling. For example, ginkgo biloba and saw palmetto can increase bleeding, which may worsen gum swelling. Licorice root can raise blood pressure, potentially interacting with medications like beta blockers. These interactions can be unpredictable, so it's essential to monitor your body's response.
faq_pregnant: It's generally a good idea to be cautious when trying herbal teas during pregnancy, especially if you're experiencing gum swelling. Some herbs can stimulate the uterus and cause issues. Chamomile and peppermint teas are often considered safe, but it's still best to check the ingredients and choose a reputable brand to minimize risks.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for gum swelling while breastfeeding. However, some herbs may pass into breast milk, so it's essential to check the ingredients. Look for teas with ingredients like peppermint or chamomile, which are generally considered safe for consumption while breastfeeding. But always read the labels carefully to avoid any potential issues.
